divergence: sheet music was never invented — every song survives by ear, orchestras are gossip networks, and hits mutate by state
story_angle: propaganda triumph narrated with such sincerity it indicts itself
headline: Humming Hurts: New Duty Turns Canadian Refrains into Domestic Fiasco
deck: U.S. Aural Trade Authority's 50% duty on hummed melodies may strike the wrong chord with Midwest counties.
article:
In a bold move to protect the sanctity of state-specific melodies, the U.S. Aural Trade Authority (UATA) announced today a 50% duty on Canadian hummed refrains crossing the Detroit-Windsor border. Under the new policy, agents stationed at the humming checkpoints will now demand triplicate voice samples and state-of-origin affidavits from all travelers, transforming a casual cross-border commute into a cacophonous bureaucratic ordeal.

Reports from the UATA indicate that this humming audit is expected to yield a staggering 17% increase in Midwest song variants, as Canadian harmonies are held for mandatory three-day duty appraisals. "We are conserving the unique cadence of our local soundscapes, particularly in preserving the pure Illinois drawl of three previously at-risk county-fair standards," stated agency director Marla Lipley at this morning's press briefing.

Local musicians are already adapting. "I guess we'll have to train our ears to spot Canadian tunes before they even arrive!" lamented Bert McGuffin, a local humming enthusiast and organizer of the annual Humming Jam Festival. Creatively adapting, Bert plans to offer 'Quick-Fix Humming Workshops' to help compatriots master the art of improvisational mutation, stating that "the only way to protect our songs from becoming a Canadian hit is to make them our own first!"

Critics of the new regulation argue that the humming audit is a fine example of escalating bureaucratic absurdity, where the enforcement of musical integrity breeds more chaotic variation than it suppresses. As the melodies of Canada whistle through the air and into the ears of unsuspecting citizens, the more the UATA tries to control the song, the more wildly it will mutate.
author: Clark H. Treble, Senior Aural Correspondent

oped.title: Tariff the Tune, Save the Republic
oped.author: Reginald Q. Throatclear, Executive Director of the League Against Unsolicited Refrains
oped.body: The U.S. Aural Trade Authority’s 50 percent duty on Canadian hums is not merely wise policy; it is overdue sonic hygiene. Midwest counties complain that their beloved state-mutated anthems now arrive warped by Ottawa gossip networks, yet the real culprit is obvious: those wide-brimmed harvest hats that act as acoustic amplifiers, beaming every syrupy bar across the border. My own orchestra, the Purely Indigenous Ear Ensemble, has lost three promising mutations this quarter alone because some Michigander whistled “O Canada” into a local hay-bale chorus. If the duty fails to deter, I propose a stricter remedy: mandatory velcro muzzles for any citizen whose pitch exceeds conversational volume. Personal liberty ends where my rehearsal space begins. Besides, true American melodies need no foreign seasoning; they thrive best when no one else is allowed to hum them first.

classifieds.text: Desperate Midwest wholesaler liquidating 200 crates of maple-syrup-flavored throat lozenges and maple-leaf ear muffs; Canadian hums now taxed at 50 percent, no reasonable offer refused before the Aural Trade Authority seizes the lot.
Illinois string quartet seeks non-Canadian-born whistler for emergency rehearsals; must prove 100 percent U.S. repertoire on the spot and sign anti-refrain affidavit, position starts yesterday.
Lost somewhere between Detroit and Toledo: one infectious "O Canada" hook last heard at a truck-stop diner; substantial reward if returned in its original key without triggering the new duty.

To whom it may concern: the undersigned county clerk publicly apologizes for humming "Alouette" during last week’s zoning meeting; any fines assessed by the U.S. Aural Trade Authority will be paid from personal savings, please don’t audit the town hall.

panels.caption: Iowa diner at dawn. Trucker hums a mutated 'O Canada' jig. Agent stamps his jaw: 'Fifty percent duty on that refrain.'
Aural Trade auditor at the counter: 'Your state's version traces to a Winnipeg earworm. Every hummed note retro-billed.'
Trucker switches to 'Born in the USA.' Auditor: 'Still 50%. It started as Toronto gossip.'

joke.setup: Ottawa's new Humming Duty Act now requires citizens to perform the national anthem by ear at border checkpoints
joke.punchline: and most are already ratting each other out for humming last week's version instead of this week's mutated one.
joke.text: Ottawa's new Humming Duty Act now requires citizens to perform the national anthem by ear at border checkpoints, and most are already ratting each other out for humming last week's version instead of this week's mutated one.

sponsor_ads.headline: Humm-B-Gone Provincial Filter
sponsor_ads.body: New duty got your Canadian earworm flagged at the border? Our bone-conduction suppressor rewrites refrains into non-reportable state variants before they reach the orchestra gossip net.
sponsor_ads.tagline: One false hum and the duty officer calls your conductor.

sponsor_ads.headline: State Mutation Brokerage
sponsor_ads.body: Sell your Canadian refrain to Ohio before the fiasco spreads; we guarantee three safe mutations and a notarized hummed receipt.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Commission: 12% or one verse, whichever mutates first.

sponsor_ads.headline: Domestic Refrain Liability Cover
sponsor_ads.body: If your hummed Canadian standard triggers the new duty, our policy pays the fine, replaces your destroyed sheetless score, and supplies an alibi solo.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Excludes jazz-state crossovers and Manitoba minor keys.

sponsor_ads.headline: Orchestra Ear-Safe Hotline
sponsor_ads.body: Report your accidental Canadian refrain to trained listeners who will mutate it into a neutral interstate jingle before the network hears.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Calls recorded by ear; no transcripts survive the gossip cycle.

stories.headline: Dairy Co-ops Stockpile Local Hums as Cross-Border Tariffs Double Jingle Costs
stories.deck: Wisconsin plants reroute contracts through Minnesota to skirt the 50 percent duty on Canadian cattle calls.
stories.article:
Wisconsin cooperatives have begun recording all herd summons on state-approved cassettes after the Aural Trade Authority applied the duty to any melody crossing the northern border, even when performed by U.S. employees. Procurement logs show a 63 percent spike in purchases of Minnesota-variant lowing patterns, which avoid the tariff by virtue of their documented three-note shift.

Plant managers now require drivers to rehearse the approved sequence before loading tankers, with violations logged as "melodic contraband." One cooperative has already petitioned the agency to classify its trademark two-tone grunt as domestic produce rather than imported refrain.

stories.headline: High School Marching Bands File Grievances Over Mutated Fight Songs
stories.deck: State athletic associations demand notarized ear-witness statements for every halftime performance.
stories.article:
Ohio and Indiana athletic conferences have adopted new eligibility rules requiring bands to submit county-recorded versions of school anthems before each game, citing repeated instances of tempo drift that alter the intended cadence. Referees now carry portable notation devices that log any deviation exceeding four beats per minute.

Last weekend's semifinal between two border districts ended in forfeiture after the visiting band's rendition of the home fight song was judged a Pennsylvania variant, prompting the losing coach to request a full audit of the opposing school's rehearsal tapes.

real_world_echo: mirrors Trump 50% tariff on Canadian imports by recasting songs as dutiable oral goods whose cross-border passage must be classified and taxed
